description_long: This is a tall, rectangular stone obelisk with a rounded top, standing vertically on a square, flat base. The surface is weathered and shows signs of age, with some rough, chipped areas near the bottom. One face of the obelisk features faint inscriptions or engravings, though they are partially eroded and difficult to read. The stone is a pale, off-white color with subtle variations in tone, and the texture appears rough and unpolished. The base is simple and unadorned, slightly wider than the obelisk itself, providing stability. The overall appearance suggests a utilitarian or commemorative function, typical of historical markers or milestones in urban environments.
description: A tall, pale stone obelisk with a rounded top and weathered surface, standing on a simple square base. One face has faint, eroded inscriptions.
description_view_0: The obelisk is seen from the side, showing a plain, weathered surface with roughness near the bottom and no visible inscriptions.
description_view_1: This view reveals the front face with faint, partially eroded inscriptions and a rectangular patch, likely where text or a plaque was once more visible.
description_view_2: Another side view, similar to view 0, with a plain, rough surface and no visible markings or inscriptions.
description_view_3: The rear face is visible, showing a darker, shadowed surface with roughness at the base and no inscriptions.
attribute_inscription: Faint, partially eroded inscriptions are visible on the front face, near the upper section. The text appears to be in a classical or Latin script, but is largely illegible due to weathering.
attribute_base_design: The base is a simple, square, flat platform made of the same stone material as the obelisk, slightly wider than the shaft, with no decorative features.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is smoothly rounded, blending seamlessly with the shaft, and lacks any decorative elements or unique features.
